tenders.title: S30081 RTM3 Mobile Phone Network Data
tenders.summary: The traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework (PCF) schemes and have
tenders.detailedDescription: The traffic models have delivered significant efficiency savings for National Highways by accelerating traffic modelling and economic appraisal across Project Control Framework (PCF) schemes and have been integral to the development of the RIS2 and RIS3 business cases. However, the current models are based on 2019 travel patterns and do not fully reflect post COVID travel behaviour. The Road Traffic Modelling 3 (RTM3) programme therefore seeks to update the models to a neutral 2025 post COVID baseline and produce refreshed forecasts, ensuring a robust and credible evidence base to support existing and emerging use cases. This project focuses on the procurement of expanded mobile phone network data to inform the development of prior matrices for RTM3, capturing detailed origin destination patterns across trip purposes, modes and user classes. The data is essential for model calibration, validation and forecasting, and consultants will be required to work proactively with those leading the Prior Matrix Technical Coordination Group to ensure consistency and coherence across the RTM3 programme.

tenders.detailedDescription: The Data Access Project (DAP) is being undertaken to address significant challenges in adult social care (ASC) related to fragmented, hard-to-access data. tenders.detailedDescription: The works relate to the repair of an existing set of external steps providing access from the Killacourt down to Towan Promenade, Newquay. The remainder appear in good condition however it will be for the bidder to make their own assessment to verify this.

tenders.title: 2024 -727 Behavioural Change Assessment for the Environment Baselining Pilot
tenders.summary: AHDB requires one supplier to assess the extent to which data, knowledge and other factors can drive behaviour change on farms for its Environment Baselining Pilot. AHDB are seeking a supplier who has
tenders.detailedDescription: AHDB requires one supplier to assess the extent to which data, knowledge and other factors can drive behaviour change on farms for its Environment Baselining Pilot. AHDB are seeking a supplier who has a track record of delivering behaviour change assessments/evaluations within the agriculture sector, primarily for the four levy sectors supported by AHDB/QMS namely, Dairy, Beef and Lamb, Cereals and Oilseeds, and Pork. There will be 3 main areas of consideration within the scope of this tender: • Understanding what drives farm management practice choices on farm. • Understanding of the drivers/motivation (together with challenges and barriers) for change • Measuring change - what are the actions that the 170 farmers intend to implement, and which ones are actioned, together with their impact. AHDB requires a supplier who can develop and implement an evaluation/assessment framework for the Environment Baselining Pilot.
